key_findings: Eval 0 (developer-project) shows strongest skill value: 6/6 vs 1/6 — baseline produces standard project structure without routing tables, CONTEXT.md files, or token budgets
Eval 1 (content-pipeline) is non-discriminating: 5/5 vs 5/5 — Sonnet already knows ICM methodology from training data and produces identical structural patterns without the skill

Eval 2 (broken-workspace) shows moderate skill value: 5/5 vs 2/5 — skill provides systematic anti-pattern diagnosis with factory/product framing; baseline diagnoses real repo issues but misses ICM-specific patterns
Eval 2 baseline diverged: agent read the actual repo files instead of responding to the hypothetical scenario, reducing comparability
Token usage: with-skill runs used FEWER tokens on average (47.4K vs 51.1K) despite richer structured output — the skill focuses the model's work
Content pipeline eval needs stronger assertions to differentiate skill value — current assertions test knowledge presence, not consistency quality
